---
title: "Research"
---

# Research

My research focuses on applying **quantitative and computational methods to ecological and conservation questions**. I combine spatial data, environmental datasets, and statistical modeling to understand how species respond to environmental change.

My work spans **marine ecosystems and alpine environments**, with a focus on predicting how climate change affects species distributions and population dynamics.

---

## Species Distribution Modeling of Pacific Rockfish

**Fredston Lab — University of California Santa Cruz**

I lead a research project comparing **statistical Species Distribution Models (SDMs)** and **mechanistic Dynamic Range Models (DRMs)** to forecast the distribution of **Shortbelly Rockfish** in California.

This research integrates:

- Bottom trawl survey data (2003–2024)
- Environmental variables
- Spatial population dynamics
- Recruitment variability

The goal is to improve predictions of **species abundance under climate change** for a key species in California’s commercial groundfish fishery.

---

## Alpine Bird Habitat Suitability Modeling

**Zavaleta Conservation Science & Solutions Lab**

I led a project analyzing historical museum and observation records to model **habitat suitability of alpine bird species in the Sierra Nevada**.

Species studied include:

- Horned Lark  
- Hermit Thrush  
- Cassin’s Finch  

Using historical records from **1939–1990**, we created a database of **2,344 observations across 105 species** and applied habitat suitability models to assess potential climate-driven distribution changes.

---

## High-Elevation Climate Change and Rosy Finch Dynamics

I collaborated with researchers studying **Gray-Crowned Rosy Finch populations** to examine ecological responses to warming at high elevations.

This work involved building spatial databases of species abundance from **1908–2018** and integrating geospatial and ecological datasets for climate impact analysis.

---

## Wildlife Ecology and Carnivore Behavior

**Wilmers Lab of Wildlife Ecology**

Worked on the **Botswana Carnivore Latrine Project**, studying how territoriality and social behavior influence habitat selection and movement patterns in large carnivores.

This research involved behavioral analysis across **30+ species of Botswana fauna**.
